extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco successfully defended his Liege-Bastogne-Liege title with a solo attack on Côte de la Redoute at 30km to go, winning by 1:06 over PIDCOCK.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL repeated his exact 2022 winning move from Côte de la Redoute at 30km, yet field still couldn't respond.
Soudal Quick-Step pre-damaged field before EVENEPOEL's attack, indicating Belgian squad learned from solo World Championship effort.
HEALY Ben attacked for podium in final kilometer knowing his inferior sprint, demonstrating tactical awareness over passive sitting.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Won despite internal team tension and negative press from management, suggesting mental resilience under pressure improved since 2022.
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: positive: Second place at 1:06 shows climbing range extended beyond typical one-day profile after Flanders success.
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: negative: Crashed out at 55 miles, eliminating anticipated head-to-head after dominating spring (Sanremo, Flanders, Amstel).
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: Third at Fleche but team decimated by positive COVID tests pre-race, reducing EF tactical options.
rider_signals.BUITRAGO Santiago: positive: Third place sprint suggests Colombian climbers can finish Monument podiums when pure puncheurs like VAN AERT absent.
